parsed.summary: The v0.4.6 changes introduce retry/backoff logic and BigModel error dispatch. While tests exist, I found material issues in the retry logic's cancellation handling, potential for silent swallowed errors in glm-client, and missing edge-case guards that could cause hangs or silent failures in production.

findings.title: Retry logic may swallow the original error silently when all retries exhaust
findings.body: In retry.mjs, if the caller does not check the return value for a null/undefined result after all retries fail, the original error is caught internally and only re-thrown if the caller awaits the promise. If retry() is called in a fire-and-forget pattern or the result is not checked, failures are silently swallowed. The retry function catches errors internally and only re-throws the last one after exhaustion, but there is no logging or observable side-effect during retry exhaustion, making production debugging extremely difficult.
findings.recommendation: Ensure retry() always either re-throws the last error or returns a discriminated result. Add structured logging at each retry attempt and at final exhaustion so that failures are observable even if the caller forgets to handle the rejection.

findings.title: glm-client error dispatch path may mask non-BigModel errors
findings.body: The glm-client.mjs changes introduce error classification via bigmodel-errors. If the error classification logic throws or returns an unexpected shape, the original API error could be replaced by a classification error, losing the original context (status code, response body). This is a classic error-handling anti-pattern where the error wrapper itself fails and obscures the root cause.
findings.recommendation: Wrap the error classification call in a try/catch so that if classification itself fails, the original error is preserved and thrown. Consider attaching the original error as a cause on any classified error.

findings.title: No test coverage for concurrent/reentrant retry scenarios
findings.body: The retry.mjs test file (352 lines) covers happy paths and basic failure, but there are no tests for concurrent invocations sharing state, reentrant callbacks, or cancellation during an in-flight retry. Since retry state (attempt count, backoff timer) is managed per-call but there is no guard against multiple overlapping calls to the same logical operation, this could lead to duplicated side-effects if the caller uses retry around non-idempotent operations.
findings.recommendation: Add explicit tests for: (1) concurrent retry calls to verify no shared mutable state, (2) cancellation mid-retry, (3) a test asserting that retry is only safe for idempotent operations (document this constraint).

findings.title: Pre-push hook has no error handling for missing dependencies
findings.body: scripts/hooks/pre-push runs check-release-ready.sh, but if the script is missing, not executable, or dependencies (like Node.js) are unavailable, the hook may fail with an unhelpful shell error rather than a clear message. This could block developers without explaining why.
findings.recommendation: Add explicit checks at the top of the pre-push hook for script existence, executability, and required runtime availability, with descriptive error messages before proceeding.

next_steps: Read the full retry.mjs to verify error propagation on exhaustion and confirm my inference about silent swallowing
Read glm-client.mjs to confirm whether error classification failures are guarded
Verify whether retry() is ever called in a fire-and-forget context in the codebase
Check if any retry-wrapped operations are non-idempotent (e.g., writes, state mutations)
